title: ruppia maritima subsp. rostrata
intro: Ruppia maritima subsp. rostrata, also known as the narrow-leaved sea lavender, is a subspecies of the aquatic plant Ruppia maritima, which is a type of seagrass found in shallow coastal waters. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are believed to aid in wound healing and the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Ruppia maritima subsp. rostrata include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and provide antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of Ruppia maritima subsp. rostrata can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams, and are often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and infections, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
